input.command:
gh pr edit 28 \
  --title "Training infrastructure upgrade: diff-aware loss, HPO, MLflow, dataset pipeline + 9-gap closure incl. round-2 distillation" \
  --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

This branch carries two major bodies of work against `main`:

**Part A — Original training infra overhaul** (see original commits before gap work began).
**Part B — 9-gap closure and round-2 hypernet distillation loop** (sessions 1-5, 30+ commits).

At HEAD the branch is production-quality: 204 tests pass, ruff clean, mypy clean across all affected modules.

---

## Part A — Training infrastructure & dataset pipeline

Comprehensive overhaul of Rune's QLoRA fine-tuning pipeline plus the dataset-pipeline work folded in.

**Training infrastructure:**
- Diff-aware loss: line-level hunk-weighted token loss via `DiffWeightedDataCollator` + `DiffAwareSFTTrainer`, with identity-under-uniform-weights regression guard
- HPO fitness overhaul: diff-restricted metrics (`hunk_loss`, `hunk_accuracy`, `adapter_improvement`, `hunk_entropy`) with task-level heldout split (no pair-level leakage)
- Training-hyperparameter Optuna study (`run_training_hpo.py`) with Hyperband pruner
- MLflow tracking wired into QLoRA path via `training_common`
- Unified `train.sh` CLI + `trainer_cli` with `--dry-run`

**Dataset pipeline (merged from retired `feat/training-dataset-pipeline`):**
- `d2l_diff.py` — RTK-style diff compression
- `d2l_data.py::normalize_mined_pairs` — per-step training pair extraction (task_id leakage guard)
- `mine_github.py --batch` mode + per-repo error handling with `mining_repos.json` config
- `pairs_to_chat_messages` SFT converter returning `(conversations, pre_post_records)`

---

## Part B — Gap closure (all 9 gaps shipped)

| # | Gap | Commit(s) |
|---|-----|-----------|
| 1 | Workspace mypy config (pandas-stubs, inference/shared/evaluation globs) | `917150e` |
| 2 | APPS stratification parity (`load_problems` delegation) | `917150e` |
| 3 | SWE-Bench-Lite `score()` (env-gated clone/apply/pytest) | `683553c` |
| 4 | Oracle validation runner (`scripts/validate_oracles.py`, ≥3% over base) | `98b8713` |
| 5 | Kill-switch wiring into hypernetwork training loop | `510464f` |
| 6 | `task_description` propagation through `_make_pair_record` | `d710005` |
| 7 | S3 manifest upload for corpus producer | `07fe208` |
| 8 | GPU-distributed corpus generation (`--shard`, `--cuda-visible-devices`) | `d1fe8a5` |
| 9 | **Round-2 hypernetwork training loop (22 commits, full new plan)** | `2a007ed`–`e74f6a5` |

Plus `cb12695` removes 8 superseded plan/spec/handoff docs from `docs/superpowers/` (their intent has shipped; history remains accessible via `git log`).

---

## Part B highlight — Round-2 hypernetwork training (gap 9)

New modules that train the Sakana HyperLoRA against **per-bin oracle adapters as teacher signals** instead of the bare base model.

### Design invariants (validated in two-stage code review per task)

1. **Functional-LoRA teacher, not PeftModel teacher.** Oracle applied to the base model via the same `apply_functional_lora` context as the student pass. Base model is never structurally mutated → no PEFT hook-leakage between passes.
2. **Cache stores `LoraDict` tensor dicts** (`{module: {A, B}}` shape), not `PeftModel` wrappers. `_load_oracle_as_lora_dict` parses a PEFT safetensors adapter and stacks per-layer A/B tensors across `hc.layer_indices`.
3. **`oracle_fallback=\"skip\"` default.** Below-coverage runs abort at startup (`min_oracle_coverage=0.8` mandatory gate). Preserves the premise that the hypernet learns only from oracle signal.
4. **Round-2 adapter registered with oracle lineage.** `adapter_id = round2_<uuid[:8]>`, `task_type=\"round2_hypernet\"`, `parent_ids = json.dumps(sorted(oracle_ids))`, `generation=2`.
5. **Strict success gate** (`evaluate_round2_gate`): ≥4 of 6 benchmarks improved by ≥2% Pass@1 AND no regression >1% on any benchmark. CLI exits 0 on PASS / 1 on FAIL.

### New files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `libs/model-training/src/model_training/round2_config.py` | `Round2TrainConfig` (inherits `D2LTrainConfig`) |
| `libs/model-training/src/model_training/oracle_cache.py` | `_bin_key_for_record`, `lookup_oracle_path`, `audit_oracle_coverage`, `_load_oracle_as_lora_dict`, `OracleAdapterCache` |
| `libs/model-training/src/model_training/round2_train.py` | `_teacher_forward_with_oracle`, `_training_step_round2`, `train_d2l_qwen3_round2`, `_run_training_loop`, `register_round2_adapter` |
| `libs/model-training/src/model_training/round2_gate.py` | `evaluate_round2_gate` + canonical thresholds |
| `scripts/train_round2.py` | Training CLI (every `Round2TrainConfig` field exposed) |
| `scripts/evaluate_round2.py` | Success-gate CLI (6-benchmark runner + strict verdict JSON) |

### TDD cadence

Every task followed RED → verify fail → GREEN → verify pass → commit. Two-stage review (spec compliance + code quality) after each task, with fix-and-re-review cycles. 12 of 22 gap-9 commits were fix commits addressing real issues caught by reviewers — the process paid for itself.

---

## Test plan

### Automated (run and green at HEAD)

- [x] `uv run pytest` — full suite covering corpus-producer, model-training (oracle_cache, round2_config, round2_train, round2_gate, d2l_train, d2l_data, kill_switch), evaluation, validate_oracles → **204 passed**
- [x] `uv run ruff check libs/ scripts/` — clean
- [x] `uv run mypy libs/model-training/src/model_training/{round2_config,oracle_cache,round2_train,round2_gate}.py scripts/train_round2.py scripts/evaluate_round2.py` — clean
- [x] `scripts/train.sh --dry-run` smoke — threads `warmup_ratio`, `override_lora_alpha/dropout`, `neftune_noise_alpha`, `diff_aware_loss`

### Operator-run (post-merge, GPU-required)

- [ ] **Round-1 oracle training.** Produce up to 25 per-bin oracle adapters via `scripts/phase_corpus_producer.py` (use `--shard` + `--cuda-visible-devices` for multi-GPU). Each registered as `oracle_<bin_key>` in `AdapterRegistry`.
- [ ] **Plan B encoder pretraining.** With gap 6 closed, retention should flip 0% → ~100%. Run the augment + training loop end-to-end.
- [ ] **Round-1 baseline benchmark report.** Execute the 6 canonical benchmarks (HumanEval, MBPP, APPS, BigCodeBench, DS-1000, LiveCodeBench) once, save Pass@1 as `round1_scores.json`.
- [ ] **Round-2 training run.** `uv run scripts/train_round2.py --sakana-checkpoint-path ... --oracle-registry-url ... --dataset-path ... --num-steps 1000 --kill-switch-enabled`.
- [ ] **Round-2 success gate.** `uv run scripts/evaluate_round2.py --round2-adapter-id round2_<hex8> --baseline-report round1_scores.json --output-report round2_verdict.json`. Gate must exit 0.
- [ ] **Oracle validation pass.** `uv run scripts/validate_oracles.py --base-model Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --oracle <bin_key>:<adapter_id> ...` per bin.
- [ ] **HPO one-trial E2E** (original Part A) — verify `eval/hunk_loss,hunk_accuracy,adapter_improvement,hunk_entropy` land in MLflow.

Entire-Checkpoint: 0495f927fe89","messageHeadline":"fix: remove dead mode field and add per-repo error handling in batch …","oid":"4bb6a0d3ea5b7439cb7baee700db0510ac1e552f"},{"authoredDate":"2026-04-01T09:55:25Z","authors":[{"email":"noah@elixirtrials.com","id":"U_kgDODYdVwQ","login":"ET-NoahDolev","name":"Noah Dolev"}],"committedDate":"2026-04-01T11:40:25Z","messageBody":"Entire-Checkpoint: d154678a297a","messageHeadline":"fix: remove trailing commas from mining_repos.json","oid":"60c51354f003ed93bb1273001c8d7ae5b5b25642"},{"authoredDate":"2026-04-21T15:20:54Z","authors":[{"email":"noah@elixirtrials.com","id":"U_kgDODYdVwQ","login":"ET-NoahDolev","name":"Noah Dolev"},{"email":"noreply@anthropic.com","id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgxODQ3","login":"claude","name":"Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)"}],"committedDate":"2026-04-21T15:20:54Z","messageBody":"Add MLflow helpers to trainer.py mirroring the pattern in d2l_train.py so
QLoRA runs produce the same tracking surface as the distillation pipeline.
Helpers are gated by RUNE_DISABLE_MLFLOW=1 and by mlflow ImportError, so
CPU-only environments and tests continue to work unchanged.

- _setup_mlflow_trainer: configure tracking URI + experiment (env fallback)
- _mlflow_log_params / _mlflow_log_artifact: silent no-ops on failure
- _mlflow_log_output_artifacts: logs adapter safetensors + config.json
- _mlflow_run context manager: start/end run, logs params, guards finally
- train_qlora / train_and_register: new mlflow_experiment and
  mlflow_tracking_uri kwargs; SFTConfig.report_to toggles to "mlflow"
  when enabled so per-step loss streams natively via TRL.

Tests: libs/model-training/tests/test_trainer_mlflow.py covers the
RUNE_DISABLE_MLFLOW path, the ImportError path, the log-helper no-op
path, and the new kwargs surface on both public functions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","messageHeadline":"feat(training): MLflow tracking in QLoRA trainer","oid":"32dc16f431070cbe94535ef7a911116089b309d3"},{"authoredDate":"2026-04-21T15:27:54Z","authors":[{"email":"noah@elixirtrials.com","id":"U_kgDODYdVwQ","login":"ET-NoahDolev","name":"Noah Dolev"},{"email":"noreply@anthropic.com","id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgxODQ3","login":"claude","name":"Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)"}],"committedDate":"2026-04-21T15:27:54Z","messageBody":"Add pairs_to_chat_messages() in d2l_data.py and wire a dataset_path branch
into train_qlora / train_and_register so QLoRA can train directly on the
JSONL emitted by scripts/mine_github.py --batch.

Multi-turn mode (default) clusters pairs by metadata.source_task_id and
emits one conversation per task ordered by step_index — this preserves the
attempt→review→correction structure the DeltaCoder warm-start already
encodes, so the fine-tune reinforces rather than flattens the trajectory.
Single-turn mode emits one [system, user, assistant] per pair as a flat
fallback when clustering information is absent.

- d2l_data.py: SYSTEM_PROMPT, pairs_to_chat_messages, _extract_revision,
  _pairs_to_single_turn, _group_pairs_by_task. Exported in __all__.
- trainer.py: new dataset_path and encoding_mode kwargs on train_qlora
  and train_and_register. session_id type widened to str | None. Dataset
  construction extracted to _build_training_dataset(dataset_cls=...)
  to keep train_qlora under the ruff C901 threshold. Guard rail in
  _validate_data_source raises early before any GPU imports.

Tests: libs/model-training/tests/test_pairs_to_chat.py — empty input,
single_turn, multi_turn clustering, step_index ordering under reverse
input, cross-task separation, metadata fallback to task_id, corrupt
record fallback, and empty-delta skip.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","messageHeadline":"feat(training): mined-pairs → SFT chat converter","oid":"1651a96fe7d1fc5a9be5c90b62cd1cab26faaa98"},{"authoredDate":"2026-04-21T15:34:03Z","authors":[{"email":"noah@elixirtrials.com","id":"U_kgDODYdVwQ","login":"ET-NoahDolev","name":"Noah Dolev"},{"email":"noreply@anthropic.com","id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgxODQ3","login":"claude","name":"Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)"}],"committedDate":"2026-04-21T15:34:03Z","messageBody":"Add a one-command shell wrapper and a CPU-safe argparse entrypoint so
QLoRA training runs can be launched from the repo root without calling
into train_and_register from Python directly. All flags map 1:1 to
train_and_register kwargs.

- libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer_cli.py: argparse
  surface (--dataset XOR --session-id, --model, --warm-start with
  aliases, --adapter-id, --epochs, --lr, --rank, --alpha, --grad-accum,
  --lr-scheduler, --encoding-mode, --experiment-name, --mlflow-uri,
  --task-type, --database-url, --dry-run). --dry-run prints a resolved
  JSON document and returns 0 without importing torch, so CI can smoke
  the flag surface.
- scripts/train.sh: thin wrapper that fixes CWD to the repo root, sets
  PYTHONPATH to the workspace src dirs (so --dry-run works without
  `uv pip install -e` on every workspace package), and execs
  `uv run python -m model_training.trainer_cli "$@"`.

Warm-start aliases: 'deltacoder' resolves to danielcherubini/Qwen3.5-
DeltaCoder-9B; 'off'/'none'/'' disable warm-start; any other string is
passed through as an HF repo id or local path.

Tests: libs/model-training/tests/test_trainer_cli.py — 7 cases cover
--dry-run JSON resolution, warm-start alias mapping, mutually-exclusive
data-source enforcement, a torch-not-imported invariant verified in a
fresh uv subprocess, and a bash-level train.sh smoke test.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","messageHeadline":"feat(training): train.sh unified wrapper + trainer_cli","oid":"56958af4b56771b24cca4899cb482e18c8209f26"},{"authoredDate":"2026-04-21T15:38:36Z","authors":[{"email":"noah@elixirtrials.com","id":"U_kgDODYdVwQ","login":"ET-NoahDolev","name":"Noah Dolev"},{"email":"noreply@anthropic.com","id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgxODQ3","login":"claude","name":"Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)"}],"committedDate":"2026-04-21T15:38:36Z","messageBody":"Introduce a diff-aware per-token loss weighter so procedural trajectory
SFT can bias gradient toward the actual delta (the reviewer's correction)
rather than unchanged boilerplate copied from the pre-revision context.
Off by default — opt in with --diff-aware-loss on train.sh.

- libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py
  - compute_diff_loss_weights: pure function over (input_ids, labels)
    emitting per-token weights. Tokens masked by TRL's assistant-only
    collator (labels == -100) get 0.0. Assistant tokens whose id
    appears anywhere in the masked context span get unchanged_weight
    (default 0.3); tokens not present get changed_weight (default 1.0).
  - DiffWeightedDataCollator: wraps an inner SFT collator and attaches
    a loss_weights float tensor to the batch. torch import deferred.
  - build_diff_aware_sft_trainer: constructs a DiffAwareSFTTrainer
    subclass whose compute_loss multiplies per-token CE by loss_weights
    before averaging over non-masked positions. Identity when
    changed_weight == unchanged_weight (regression guard).

- libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer.py: new
  diff_aware_loss, diff_changed_weight, diff_unchanged_weight kwargs
  on both train_qlora and train_and_register. The diff-aware branch
  builds the subclassed trainer, wraps its auto-constructed collator
  with DiffWeightedDataCollator, and sets SFTConfig.assistant_only_loss
  = False (the custom collator subsumes the masking). Vanilla path
  unchanged for default runs.

- libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer_cli.py: expose
  --diff-aware-loss, --diff-changed-weight, --diff-unchanged-weight.

- libs/model-training/tests/test_diff_loss.py: 10 cases — empty input,
  masked-position zeros, context-token weight mapping, identity under
  uniform weights, length mismatch, all-masked edge case, no-context
  edge case, CPU-importability invariant, trainer_cli flag pass-through,
  and default-off verification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>","messageHeadline":"feat(training): diff-aware loss collator and trainer subclass","oid":"49a1a8a617d95ef0db819a8d18bf7e938571a3c6"},{"authoredDate":"2026-04-21T15:45:55Z","authors":[{"email":"noah@elixirtrials.com","id":"U_kgDODYdVwQ","login":"ET-NoahDolev","name":"Noah Dolev"},{"email":"noreply@anthropic.com","id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgxODQ3","login":"claude","name":"Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)"}],"committedDate":"2026-04-21T15:45:55Z","messageBody":"Add an Optuna-based HPO harness targeting *training*-time hyperparameters
of the DeltaCoder warm-start QLoRA fine-tune on mined trajectory data.
Complements the existing inference-side optimizer (run_optimization.py).

Warm-start-aware search space:
- lr (log-uniform, 1e-5 ... 5e-4)
- alpha_override (categorical {16, 32, 64, 128}) — applied post-load
- lora_dropout (categorical {0.0, 0.05, 0.1}) — applied post-load
- warmup_ratio (uniform 0 ... 0.1)
- grad_accum (categorical {8, 16, 32})
- lr_scheduler (categorical {constant, cosine})
- diff_aware_loss (categorical {False, True}) — lets HPO adjudicate
  whether the diff-weighted collator from the previous commit wins.

rank and target_modules are NOT in the search space because they're
baked into the DeltaCoder safetensor shapes. Alpha and dropout ARE
tunable at runtime — PEFT caches both per-layer, so mutating
peft_config post-load does not propagate. Two new helpers walk the
module tree and patch the cached values:

- libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer.py:
  _override_lora_alpha updates layer.scaling[adapter] = alpha / r on
  every LoraLayer in the model. _override_lora_dropout updates
  layer.lora_dropout[adapter].p. Both are called once after
  PeftModel.from_pretrained when the corresponding kwarg is set.
  New optional kwargs override_lora_alpha / override_lora_dropout
  on train_qlora and train_and_register (threaded through
  trainer_cli.py flags are deferred to a follow-up — HPO passes them
  via direct train_and_register calls).

- Trainer construction extracted to _construct_sft_trainer to keep
  train_qlora under the ruff C901 threshold.

- scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py: Optuna HyperbandPruner +
  TPESampler study. Subsampled proxy trials (500 records × 1 epoch) so
  an overnight study on a single L4 24GB lands in ~8-14 GPU-hours.
  Per-trial adapters live under ./hpo_artifacts/<study>/trial_N/;
  a retention pass after study completion keeps top-K by fitness.
  Fitness is a blended
  0.6 * (1 - normalized_eval_loss) + 0.4 * pass_at_1_humaneval_smoke
  with min-max normalization across completed trials and a midpoint
  fallback for the first 3 trials. --print-only prints the study plan
  without importing optuna or running trials (CI-safe).

Tests:
- libs/model-training/tests/test_lora_overrides.py: 8 cases covering
  alpha/dropout helpers against a minimal no-torch fake PEFT model,
  including no-op-on-unknown-adapter and robust-to-non-lora-modules
  edge cases. Also asserts the new trainer kwargs are exposed.
- scripts/optimization/tests/test_training_hpo.py: 10 cases covering
  argparse defaults, --print-only path (asserts optuna NOT imported),
  --smoke budget shrink, subsample helper, fitness blend math
  (midpoint fallback, reward direction, clamping, inf loss handling),
  and the trial-param → train_and_register kwargs translation.

Entire-Checkpoint: bf872718a5fb","messageHeadline":"feat(training): thread pre/post columns and tokenizer into diff-aware…","oid":"fb6cc2dfed99d21d9c499cf6d2297936345888f5"},{"authoredDate":"2026-04-22T08:17:55Z","authors":[{"email":"noah@elixirtrials.com","id":"U_kgDODYdVwQ","login":"ET-NoahDolev","name":"Noah Dolev"},{"email":"noreply@anthropic.com","id":"MDQ6VXNlcjgxODQ3","login":"claude","name":"Claude Opus 4.7"}],"committedDate":"2026-04-22T08:17:55Z","messageBody":"Replace the HumanEval stub with _evaluate_adapter_on_heldout — a
teacher-forced forward pass that computes NLL, top-1 accuracy, and
predictive entropy restricted to + / replace hunk tokens. A second
pass with the adapter disabled yields the adapter_improvement delta.

Fitness formula:
  w_L * (1 - norm(hunk_loss)) + w_A * hunk_accuracy
  + w_D * max(0, adapter_improvement)

Defaults (0.5 / 0.3 / 0.2) rebalance to (0.6 / 0.4 / 0.0) when
--no-adapter-improvement-eval disables the second pass.

New CLI flags: --hunk-loss-weight, --hunk-accuracy-weight,
--adapter-improvement-weight, --adapter-improvement-eval (BooleanOptional,
default on), --heldout-fraction (0.1), --heldout-strategy
(step_index default | random). Removed: --eval-tier,
--loss-weight, --pass-at-1-weight.

_stratify_heldout_split guarantees no task_id leak between train and
eval — step_index holds out each sampled task's terminal revision;
random moves entire tasks.

--print-only still avoids importing optuna.

### scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py:726


1\. shutil.rmtree() deletes adapters 📘 Rule violation ≡ Correctness


The HPO retention logic hard-deletes trial adapter directories via shutil.rmtree, which violates
write-once/archival semantics for LoRA adapter artifacts. This can permanently remove adapters
without an archive trail or versioning safeguards.




Agent Prompt

```
## Issue description
`scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py` deletes LoRA adapter artifacts with `shutil.rmtree`, violating the write-once + archive/soft-delete requirement.

## Issue Context
Even if these adapters are “trial artifacts”, they are still LoRA adapter outputs and must be retained immutably and/or archived rather than hard-deleted.

## Fix Focus Areas
- scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py[708-726]
```

### libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py:418


7\. Fallback ignores diff weights 🐞 Bug ≡ Correctness


DiffWeightedDataCollator’s non-hunk path always calls compute_diff_loss_weights with
changed_ids=set(), so it never reflects any actual diff and silently reduces to a uniform rescale.
This contradicts the warning/docstring that claims a set-based diff fallback and can materially
change training when side-channels are absent (e.g., session_id datasets).




Agent Prompt

```
### Issue description
`DiffWeightedDataCollator.__call__` logs that it is “falling back to set-based diff loss weights”, but then calls `compute_diff_loss_weights(... changed_ids=set() ...)`, which makes the fallback effectively ignore diffs.

### Issue Context
This triggers whenever `pre_code`/`post_code` side-channels are missing or a tokenizer isn’t set.

### Fix Focus Areas
- libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py[381-422]

### Suggested fix
- Replace the `changed_ids=set()` fallback with a real computation.
  - If `pre_code`/`post_code` are available, compute a changed-id set from them (tokenize both and take a set difference) and pass it to `compute_diff_loss_weights`.
```

content.content:
### scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py:467


8\. Hpo eval runs on cpu 🐞 Bug ➹ Performance


The heldout evaluator in run_training_hpo.py loads the base model with
AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(base_model_id) without device_map/quantization and then moves
tensors to model.device, which is CPU by default. This makes heldout evaluation infeasible for a 9B
model and can dominate or stall HPO runs.




Agent Prompt

```
### Issue description
Heldout evaluation loads and runs the model on CPU, which is prohibitively slow (and likely memory-infeasible) for the intended 9B HPO loop.

### Issue Context
The training path already uses `BitsAndBytesConfig` + `device_map="auto"` to run efficiently.

### Fix Focus Areas
- scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py[452-497]
- libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer.py[507-524]

### Suggested fix
- Mirror the training loader setup in `_evaluate_adapter_on_heldout`:
  - Pass `device_map="auto"` to `AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained`.
  - Consider using the same 4-bit quantization config (NF4) and `torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16`.
  - Ensure the tokenizer/model are compatible with the PEFT adapter loading.
- Also pass `attention_mask` from the tokenizer call into the forward pass to be safe if padding is introduced later.
```

input.prompt:
CI is failing on PR #28 with 24 ruff errors in `tests/corpus_producer/`. Fix them all and commit.

Working directory: `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune`.

## Conventions

- `uv run` for all commands.
- ruff line-length 88.
- Conventional commits with `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>` trailer.

## Errors to fix

Run `uv run ruff check tests/corpus_producer/` to see them all. Summary:

- `tests/corpus_producer/test_binning.py:19:89` — E501 line too long
- `tests/corpus_producer/test_integration.py:27` + `:28` — I001 import-block-unformatted
- `tests/corpus_producer/test_integration.py` — 15× E501 (lines 92, 97, 99, 115, 120, 125, 135, 142, 172, 179, 202, 230, 235, 249, 256)
- `tests/corpus_producer/test_pipeline_runner.py` — 4× E501 (lines 29, 38, 44, 57)
- `tests/corpus_producer/test_rationalization.py:102` — F841 unused `new = star_rationalize(...)` assignment
- `tests/corpus_producer/test_rationalization.py:124` — E501

## Approach

For each E501, wrap the long line using one of:
- Splitting function call args across multiple lines.
- Breaking a long string across adjacent string literals.
- Extracting a local variable for a long expression.

For F841 `new = star_rationalize(...)` at line 102 of `test_rationalization.py`: the variable is unused. Either
- Replace `new = star_rationalize(...)` with a bare `star_rationalize(...)` call (if the return value isn't needed in the test body), OR
- If the test intends to assert against `new`, add the missing assertion.

Read the function context before deciding. If there's no assertion against `new` anywhere in the test function, drop the assignment.

For I001 import-block-unformatted at `test_integration.py:27-28`: run `uv run ruff check --fix tests/corpus_producer/test_integration.py` — if it doesn't auto-fix, manually reorder the imports in the block.

## Verification

- `uv run ruff check tests/corpus_producer/` → `All checks passed!`
- `uv run pytest tests/corpus_producer/ libs/corpus-producer/tests/` → all tests pass (should be same count as before ~74+).

## Commit

```bash
git add tests/corpus_producer/
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
style(tests): fix ruff E501/F841/I001 in tests/corpus_producer/

CI (lint-and-type-check) was failing on 24 ruff issues in the corpus
producer test files, left behind from earlier gap 7/8 work. Mechanical
fixes only — no test logic changed.

- E501: wrap lines >88 chars across function arg splits + adjacent
  string literals.
- F841: drop unused `new = star_rationalize(...)` assignment in
  test_rationalization.py where no subsequent assertion uses `new`.
- I001: reorder imports in test_integration.py.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

Don't touch anything outside `tests/corpus_producer/`.

Report: status, commit SHA, test count, any concerns (especially on the F841 fix — did you verify `new` was truly unused, or add the missing assertion?).

content.text:
Done.

**Status:** All 24 ruff errors fixed, commit `b81964e`.

**Test count:** 76 passed (same corpus-producer suite, no regressions).

**F841 verification:** `new` in `test_rationalize_stops_when_bin_full` was genuinely unused — the test only asserts `len(calls) == 1` (verifying the early-stop behavior), never references the return value. Dropped the assignment cleanly.

**E402 note:** The task description said I001 (import-block-unformatted) but ruff actually flagged E402 (module-level import not at top of file) on lines 27–28 of `test_integration.py`. Those imports intentionally follow a `sys.path.insert` guard, so I added `# noqa: E402` comments rather than reordering them, which would break the import at runtime.

- `tests/corpus_producer/test_rationalization.py:124` — E501

## Approach

For each E501, wrap the long line using one of:
- Splitting function call args across multiple lines.
- Breaking a long string across adjacent string literals.
- Extracting a local variable for a long expression.

For F841 `new = star_rationalize(...)` at line 102 of `test_rationalization.py`: the variable is unused. Either
- Replace `new = star_rationalize(...)` with a bare `star_rationalize(...)` call (if the return value isn't needed in the test body), OR
- If the test intends to assert against `new`, add the missing assertion.

Read the function context before deciding. If there's no assertion against `new` anywhere in the test function, drop the assignment.

For I001 import-block-unformatted at `test_integration.py:27-28`: run `uv run ruff check --fix tests/corpus_producer/test_integration.py` — if it doesn't auto-fix, manually reorder the imports in the block.

```bash
style(tests): fix ruff E501/F841/I001 in tests/corpus_producer/

CI (lint-and-type-check) was failing on 24 ruff issues in the corpus
```

file.content:
global _HUNK_FALLBACK_WARNED

        # Pop side-channel columns before handing to the inner collator.
        pre_codes: list[str | None] = []
        post_codes: list[str | None] = []
        for feat in features:
            pre_codes.append(feat.pop("pre_code", None))
            post_codes.append(feat.pop("post_code", None))

        batch = self.inner_collator(features)

        # Determine which path to use.
        has_side_channels = all(p is not None for p in pre_codes) and all(
            p is not None for p in post_codes
        )
        use_hunk_path = has_side_channels and self.tokenizer is not None

        if not use_hunk_path and not _HUNK_FALLBACK_WARNED:
            logger.warning(
                "DiffWeightedDataCollator: pre_code/post_code side-channels missing "
                "or tokenizer not set; falling back to set-based diff loss weights. "
                "Pass tokenizer= and include pre_code/post_code in dataset features "
                "to use the hunk path."
            )
            _HUNK_FALLBACK_WARNED = True

        # Defer torch import — must stay importable in CPU-only CI.
        import torch

        input_ids_batch: Any = batch["input_ids"]
        labels_batch: Any = batch["labels"]

        all_weights: list[list[float]] = []
        for idx in range(len(features)):
            ids_seq: list[int] = input_ids_batch[idx].tolist()
            lab_seq: list[int] = labels_batch[idx].tolist()
            pre = str(pre_codes[idx]) if pre_codes[idx] is not None else ""
            post = str(post_codes[idx]) if post_codes[idx] is not None else ""

            if use_hunk_path:
                w = self._weights_via_hunk_path(ids_seq, lab_seq, pre, post)
            else:
                w = compute_diff_loss_weights(
                    input_ids=ids_seq,
                    labels=lab_seq,
                    changed_ids=set(),
                    changed_weight=self.changed_weight,
                    unchanged_weight=self.unchanged_weight,
                )
            all_weights.append(w)

        batch["loss_weights"] = torch.tensor(all_weights, dtype=torch.float32)
        return batch


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Trainer factory
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

file.content:
# Deferred GPU imports keep the module CPU-importable (INFRA-05).
    import math  # noqa: PLC0415

    import torch  # noqa: PLC0415
    from model_training.d2l_data import (  # noqa: PLC0415
        _extract_post_revision,
        _extract_pre_revision,
    )
    from model_training.diff_loss import _compute_hunk_ranges  # noqa: PLC0415
    from peft import PeftModel  # noqa: PLC0415
    from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer  # noqa: PLC0415

    tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(base_model_id)
    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(base_model_id)
    adapter_model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(base_model, adapter_path)
    adapter_model.eval()

    def _forward_hunk_metrics(model: Any, disable: bool) -> tuple[float, float, float, int]:
        total_loss = 0.0
        total_acc = 0.0
        total_ent = 0.0
        total_tok = 0

        cm = model.disable_adapter() if disable else _NullContext()
        with cm, torch.no_grad():
            for pair in pairs:
                act = pair.get("activation_text", "")
                teach = pair.get("teacher_text", "")
                pre = _extract_pre_revision(act)
                post = _extract_post_revision(act, teach)
                if not post:
                    continue
                hunks = _compute_hunk_ranges(pre, post)
                if not hunks:
                    continue
                post_start = teach.rfind(post)
                if post_start == -1:
                    continue
                shifted = [(s + post_start, e + post_start) for s, e in hunks]

                enc = tokenizer(teach, return_offsets_mapping=True, return_tensors="pt")
                input_ids = enc["input_ids"].to(model.device)
                offsets = enc["offset_mapping"][0].tolist()

                logits = model(input_ids=input_ids).logits[0]
                shift_logits = logits[:-1]
                shift_ids = input_ids[0][1:]
                log_probs = torch.log_softmax(shift_logits, dim=-1)
                probs = log_probs.exp()

input.prompt:
Address 7 PR review comments on PR #28. Make 2 separate commits.

Working directory: `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune`.

Conventions: ruff line-length 88, Google docstrings, `uv run` for every command, Co-Authored-By trailer on commits.

## Commit 1 — Bug fixes (items 7, 8)

### Bug 7: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py:412-418`

The non-hunk fallback path calls `compute_diff_loss_weights(changed_ids=set(), ...)` which silently reduces to uniform unchanged_weight for all labeled tokens. The warning says "set-based diff loss weights" but no diff is computed.

Read the file first, specifically lines 380-425.

**Fix:** when `use_hunk_path` is False, replace the current `compute_diff_loss_weights(changed_ids=set(), ...)` with identity weights — 1.0 for non-IGNORE_INDEX labels, 0.0 for IGNORE_INDEX positions. This way enabling diff-aware loss doesn't silently change the training objective when the diff can't be computed.

Look for `IGNORE_INDEX` constant (or `-100` if that's the convention) in `diff_loss.py` or its imports. If not exported, define/use the transformers `-100` convention.

Replace:
```python
            if use_hunk_path:
                w = self._weights_via_hunk_path(ids_seq, lab_seq, pre, post)
            else:
                w = compute_diff_loss_weights(
                    input_ids=ids_seq,
                    labels=lab_seq,
                    changed_ids=set(),
                    changed_weight=self.changed_weight,
                    unchanged_weight=self.unchanged_weight,
                )
```

With:
```python
            if use_hunk_path:
                w = self._weights_via_hunk_path(ids_seq, lab_seq, pre, post)
            else:
                # Identity fallback: 1.0 for labeled tokens, 0.0 for IGNORE_INDEX.
                # Avoids silently reducing the training objective to a uniform
                # rescale when the diff cannot be computed (no side-channels
                # or no tokenizer).
                w = [1.0 if lab != -100 else 0.0 for lab in lab_seq]
```

Also update the warning message at lines 388-393 — change "falling back to set-based diff loss weights" to "falling back to identity loss weights (1.0 for labeled tokens, 0.0 otherwise)" so log output matches reality.

### Bug 8: `scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py:463-467`

The HPO heldout evaluator loads the 9B base model with no `device_map`/quantization and no `attention_mask`, making the eval run on CPU.

Read the file first, specifically lines 452-500 and look at how the training path loads the model for comparison (probably around lines 500-530 in trainer.py, which qodo references).

**Fix:** mirror the training loader. Add `device_map="auto"`, 4-bit NF4 `BitsAndBytesConfig`, `torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16`, and pass `attention_mask` into the forward pass.

New imports inside the function (keep INFRA-05 deferred pattern):
```python
    from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig  # noqa: PLC0415
```

Replace the base model load:
```python
    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(base_model_id)
```

With:
```python
    bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
        load_in_4bit=True,
        bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16,
        bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",
        bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,
    )
    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
        base_model_id,
        quantization_config=bnb_config,
        device_map="auto",
        torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16,
    )
```

Also find the tokenizer call inside `_forward_hunk_metrics` at line ~492:
```python
                enc = tokenizer(teach, return_offsets_mapping=True, return_tensors="pt")
                input_ids = enc["input_ids"].to(model.device)
```

Update to also pass attention_mask to the model forward:
```python
                enc = tokenizer(teach, return_offsets_mapping=True, return_tensors="pt")
                input_ids = enc["input_ids"].to(model.device)
                attention_mask = enc["attention_mask"].to(model.device)
```

And update the forward call at line ~496:
```python
                logits = model(input_ids=input_ids).logits[0]
```

To:
```python
                logits = model(input_ids=input_ids, attention_mask=attention_mask).logits[0]
```

### Verification for commit 1

- `uv run ruff check libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py` → clean
- `uv run mypy libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py` → clean
- Run any existing diff_loss tests to confirm no regression:
  - `uv run pytest libs/model-training/tests/test_diff_loss.py -v` → expect unchanged pass count
- HPO test file may have no CPU-safe tests for the eval fn; check `libs/model-training/tests/` for anything referencing `_forward_hunk_metrics` or `_evaluate_adapter_on_heldout`. If tests exist, run them.

### Commit 1

```bash
git add libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(training): address two correctness bugs flagged in PR #28 review

1. diff_loss.py: DiffWeightedDataCollator's non-hunk fallback was
   calling compute_diff_loss_weights with changed_ids=set(), which
   silently reduced the objective to a uniform unchanged_weight rescale
   instead of the advertised "set-based diff" fallback. Replaced with
   identity weights (1.0 for labeled tokens, 0.0 for IGNORE_INDEX) so
   enabling diff-aware loss without side-channels is a no-op on the
   objective rather than a silent regression. Warning message updated
   to match.

2. run_training_hpo.py: _evaluate_adapter_on_heldout loaded the 9B
   base model with no device_map / quantization, forcing CPU eval and
   stalling HPO runs. Now mirrors the training path with 4-bit NF4
   BitsAndBytesConfig + device_map="auto" + bfloat16, and passes
   attention_mask through the forward call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

## Commit 2 — Docstring + line-length fixes (items 2, 3, 4, 5, 6)

### Item 2: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/training_common.py:53-61`

`mlflow_log_params` public function lacks Args/Returns/Raises sections. Read the file, find the function, expand its docstring to Google style with the structured sections. If it takes no args (unlikely for a `log_params` function), at minimum document what it does and explicit `Returns: None.` / `Raises: None.` sections.

### Item 3: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer_cli.py:241-255`

`main()` docstring lacks structured sections. Read the function signature (likely `main(argv: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> int`), expand docstring with Args/Returns/Raises.

### Item 4: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_data.py:928-970`

`pairs_to_chat_messages` public function has Args + Returns but no Raises section. Read the function and add an explicit `Raises:` section. If it genuinely doesn't raise, the section should say so (e.g. `Raises: None.` or document any implicit exceptions like `KeyError` from dict access).

### Item 5: `scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py:736-739`

HPO `main()` docstring lacks structured sections. Same treatment as Item 3.

### Item 6: `scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py:695-703`

A `logger.info(...)` format string exceeds 88 chars. Wrap it across adjacent string literals inside parentheses. Read the file first to find the exact call and wrap it cleanly.

### Verification for commit 2

- `uv run ruff check libs/ scripts/` → clean
- `uv run mypy libs/model-training/src/model_training/training_common.py libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer_cli.py libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_data.py` → clean

### Commit 2

```bash
git add libs/model-training/src/model_training/training_common.py \
        libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer_cli.py \
        libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_data.py \
        scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
docs(training): add structured docstrings + wrap long log line (PR #28 review)

Five quality items from qodo review on PR #28:
- training_common.mlflow_log_params: add Args/Returns/Raises sections.
- trainer_cli.main: add Args/Returns/Raises sections.
- d2l_data.pairs_to_chat_messages: add Raises section.
- run_training_hpo.main: add Args/Returns/Raises sections.
- run_training_hpo logger.info line wrapped to stay under 88 chars.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

## Final verification

After both commits:
- `uv run ruff check libs/ scripts/ tests/` → `All checks passed!`
- `uv run pytest libs/model-training/tests/test_diff_loss.py libs/model-training/tests/test_training_common.py libs/model-training/tests/test_trainer_cli.py libs/model-training/tests/test_d2l_data.py -v` → all pass.

Don't touch `docs/superpowers/` or anything outside the listed files. Report both commit SHAs, test counts, any concerns.

Updated warning message to say "identity loss weights". Test `test_collator_falls_back_on_missing_pre_post` updated to assert the new 0.0/1.0 values instead of checking that the old legacy function was called.

**Fix:** mirror the training loader. Add `device_map="auto"`, 4-bit NF4 `BitsAndBytesConfig`, `torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16`, and pass `attention_mask` into the forward pass.

New imports inside the function (keep INFRA-05 deferred pattern):
```python
    from transformers import BitsAndBytesConfig  # noqa: PLC0415
```

Replace the base model load:
```python
    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(base_model_id)
```

With:
```python
    bnb_config = BitsAndBytesConfig(
        load_in_4bit=True,
        bnb_4bit_compute_dtype=torch.bfloat16,
        bnb_4bit_quant_type="nf4",
        bnb_4bit_use_double_quant=True,
    )
    base_model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(
        base_model_id,
        quantization_config=bnb_config,
        device_map="auto",
        torch_dtype=torch.bfloat16,
    )
```

Also find the tokenizer call inside `_forward_hunk_metrics` at line ~492:
```python
                enc = tokenizer(teach, return_offsets_mapping=True, return_tensors="pt")
                input_ids = enc["input_ids"].to(model.device)
```

Update to also pass attention_mask to the model forward:
```python
                enc = tokenizer(teach, return_offsets_mapping=True, return_tensors="pt")
                input_ids = enc["input_ids"].to(model.device)
                attention_mask = enc["attention_mask"].to(model.device)
```

And update the forward call at line ~496:
```python
                logits = model(input_ids=input_ids).logits[0]
```

To:
```python
                logits = model(input_ids=input_ids, attention_mask=attention_mask).logits[0]
```

### Verification for commit 1

- `uv run ruff check libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py` → clean
- `uv run mypy libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py` → clean
- Run any existing diff_loss tests to confirm no regression:
  - `uv run pytest libs/model-training/tests/test_diff_loss.py -v` → expect unchanged pass count
- HPO test file may have no CPU-safe tests for the eval fn; check `libs/model-training/tests/` for anything referencing `_forward_hunk_metrics` or `_evaluate_adapter_on_heldout`. If tests exist, run them.

### Commit 1

```bash
git add libs/model-training/src/model_training/diff_loss.py scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(training): address two correctness bugs flagged in PR #28 review

1. diff_loss.py: DiffWeightedDataCollator's non-hunk fallback was
   calling compute_diff_loss_weights with changed_ids=set(), which
   silently reduced the objective to a uniform unchanged_weight rescale
   instead of the advertised "set-based diff" fallback. Replaced with
   identity weights (1.0 for labeled tokens, 0.0 for IGNORE_INDEX) so
   enabling diff-aware loss without side-channels is a no-op on the
   objective rather than a silent regression. Warning message updated
   to match.

2. run_training_hpo.py: _evaluate_adapter_on_heldout loaded the 9B
   base model with no device_map / quantization, forcing CPU eval and
   stalling HPO runs. Now mirrors the training path with 4-bit NF4
   BitsAndBytesConfig + device_map="auto" + bfloat16, and passes
   attention_mask through the forward call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

## Commit 2 — Docstring + line-length fixes (items 2, 3, 4, 5, 6)

### Item 2: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/training_common.py:53-61`

`mlflow_log_params` public function lacks Args/Returns/Raises sections. Read the file, find the function, expand its docstring to Google style with the structured sections. If it takes no args (unlikely for a `log_params` function), at minimum document what it does and explicit `Returns: None.` / `Raises: None.` sections.

### Item 3: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer_cli.py:241-255`

`main()` docstring lacks structured sections. Read the function signature (likely `main(argv: Sequence[str] | None = None) -> int`), expand docstring with Args/Returns/Raises.

### Item 4: `libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_data.py:928-970`

`pairs_to_chat_messages` public function has Args + Returns but no Raises section. Read the function and add an explicit `Raises:` section. If it genuinely doesn't raise, the section should say so (e.g. `Raises: None.` or document any implicit exceptions like `KeyError` from dict access).

### Item 5: `scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py:736-739`

HPO `main()` docstring lacks structured sections. Same treatment as Item 3.

### Item 6: `scripts/optimization/run_training_hpo.py:695-703`

A `logger.info(...)` format string exceeds 88 chars. Wrap it across adjacent string literals inside parentheses. Read the file first to find the exact call and wrap it cleanly.

- `uv run ruff check libs/ scripts/` → clean
- `uv run mypy libs/model-training/src/model_training/training_common.py libs/model-training/src/model_training/trainer_cli.py libs/model-training/src/model_training/d2l_data.py` → clean

After both commits:

- `diff_loss.py`: replaced `compute_diff_loss_weights(changed_ids=set(), ...)` fallback with `[1.0 if lab != -100 else 0.0 for lab in lab_seq]`. Updated warning message to say "identity loss weights". Test `test_collator_falls_back_on_missing_pre_post` updated to assert the new 0.0/1.0 values instead of checking that the old legacy function was called.
- `run_training_hpo.py`: added `BitsAndBytesConfig` import (deferred, INFRA-05), wrapped base model load with 4-bit NF4 + `device_map="auto"` + `bfloat16`, threaded `attention_mask` through the tokenizer call and model forward.

def _evaluate_one(
    base_model: str,
    bin_key: str,
    benchmark: str,
    adapter_id: str,
    threshold: float,
    max_samples: int | None,
    timeout_s: int,
    workers: int,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Evaluate a single oracle against the base. Heavy imports deferred."""
    from evaluation.benchmarks import BenchmarkConfig, run_benchmark
    from evaluation.benchmarks.adapter_stack import load_adapter_stack

    cfg = BenchmarkConfig(
        timeout_s=timeout_s,
        max_workers=workers,
        max_samples=max_samples,
    )

    base_stack = load_adapter_stack(base_model=base_model, adapter_ids=[])
    stack = load_adapter_stack(base_model=base_model, adapter_ids=[adapter_id])

    base_result = run_benchmark(
        adapter_stack=base_stack,
        benchmark_id=benchmark,
        max_samples=max_samples,
        config=cfg,
    )
    stack_result = run_benchmark(
        adapter_stack=stack,
        benchmark_id=benchmark,
        max_samples=max_samples,
        config=cfg,
    )
    delta = stack_result.pass_at_1 - base_result.pass_at_1
    return {
        "bin_key": bin_key,
        "benchmark": benchmark,
        "adapter_id": adapter_id,
        "base_pass_at_1": base_result.pass_at_1,
        "stack_pass_at_1": stack_result.pass_at_1,
        "delta": delta,
        "threshold": threshold,
        "passed": delta >= threshold,

def _build_parser() -> argparse.ArgumentParser:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="validate_oracles",
        description=(
            "Per-oracle 'beat base by >=THRESHOLD' validator over run_benchmark."
        ),
        formatter_class=argparse.ArgumentDefaultsHelpFormatter,
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--base-model",
        required=True,
        help="Base HF model repo id (e.g. Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--oracle",
        action="append",
        required=True,
        metavar="BIN_KEY:ADAPTER_ID",
        help="Repeatable. Each oracle to validate.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--threshold",
        type=float,
        default=DEFAULT_THRESHOLD,
        help="Absolute Pass@1 improvement required (default: 0.03 = 3 pts).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--max-samples",
        type=int,
        default=None,
        help="Cap per-benchmark problem count.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--timeout",
        type=int,
        default=30,
        help="Per-problem timeout (seconds).",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--workers",
        type=int,
        default=4,
        help="ThreadPoolExecutor worker count.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--output",
        type=Path,
        default=None,
        help="Optional JSON results path.",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--dry-run",
        action="store_true",
        help="Skip model loading; emit a stub result per oracle.",
    )
    return parser

def validate_oracles(
    base_model: str,
    oracle_specs: list[str],
    *,
    threshold: float = DEFAULT_THRESHOLD,
    max_samples: int | None = None,
    timeout_s: int = 30,
    workers: int = 4,
    dry_run: bool = False,
) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
    """Validate a list of oracle specs and return per-oracle results.

    Args:
        base_model: Base HF repo id.
        oracle_specs: List of "<bin_key>:<adapter_id>" strings.
        threshold: Absolute Pass@1 delta required.
        max_samples: Per-benchmark cap.
        timeout_s: Per-problem timeout.
        workers: ThreadPoolExecutor workers.
        dry_run: If True, emit stub results without loading models.

    Returns:
        List of result dicts, one per oracle.
    """
    results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    for spec in oracle_specs:
        bin_key, benchmark, adapter_id = _parse_oracle_spec(spec)
        if dry_run:
            results.append(_dry_run_result(bin_key, benchmark, adapter_id, threshold))
            continue
        results.append(
            _evaluate_one(
                base_model=base_model,
                bin_key=bin_key,
                benchmark=benchmark,
                adapter_id=adapter_id,
                threshold=threshold,
                max_samples=max_samples,
                timeout_s=timeout_s,
                workers=workers,
            )
        )
    return results


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    """CLI entry point."""
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=logging.INFO, format="%(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s"
    )
    args = _build_parser().parse_args(argv)

    results = validate_oracles(
        base_model=args.base_model,
        oracle_specs=args.oracle,
        threshold=args.threshold,
        max_samples=args.max_samples,
        timeout_s=args.timeout,
        workers=args.workers,
        dry_run=args.dry_run,
    )

    if args.output:
        args.output.write_text(json.dumps(results, indent=2))
        logger.info("Wrote %d results to %s", len(results), args.output)

    n_passed = sum(1 for r in results if r["passed"])
    print(f"Oracles validated: {n_passed}/{len(results)} PASS (>= {args.threshold})")
    for r in results:
        marker = "PASS" if r["passed"] else "FAIL"
        print(
            f"  [{marker}] {r['bin_key']:<30s} delta={r['delta']:+.3f} "
            f"(base={r['base_pass_at_1']:.3f}, stack={r['stack_pass_at_1']:.3f})"
        )

    return 0 if n_passed == len(results) else 1


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(main())

file.content:
def test_parse_oracle_spec_rejects_bad_bin_key() -> None:
    """Bin keys that are neither <phase>_<benchmark> nor diagnose_pooled fail."""
    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="<phase>_<benchmark>"):
        _MOD._parse_oracle_spec("justone:adapter-1")


def test_dry_run_produces_stub_results() -> None:
    """--dry-run emits one stub per spec without loading models."""
    results = _MOD.validate_oracles(
        base_model="Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B",
        oracle_specs=[
            "decompose_humaneval:adapter-a",
            "diagnose_pooled:adapter-b",
        ],
        dry_run=True,
    )
    assert len(results) == 2
    assert all(r["dry_run"] is True for r in results)
    assert results[0]["bin_key"] == "decompose_humaneval"
    assert results[1]["bin_key"] == "diagnose_pooled"


def test_validate_oracles_marks_pass_when_delta_exceeds_threshold(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    """When stack beats base by >= threshold, oracle is marked passed."""

    def fake_eval(**_kwargs: object) -> dict[str, object]:
        return {
            "bin_key": "decompose_humaneval",
            "benchmark": "humaneval",
            "adapter_id": "adapter-x",
            "base_pass_at_1": 0.50,
            "stack_pass_at_1": 0.55,
            "delta": 0.05,
            "threshold": 0.03,
            "passed": True,
            "dry_run": False,
        }

    monkeypatch.setattr(_MOD, "_evaluate_one", fake_eval)
    results = _MOD.validate_oracles(
        base_model="Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B",
        oracle_specs=["decompose_humaneval:adapter-x"],
        threshold=0.03,
    )
    assert results[0]["passed"] is True
    assert results[0]["delta"] == 0.05

input.prompt:
Fix 4 mypy errors in `scripts/validate_oracles.py`:

```
scripts/validate_oracles.py:169: error: Missing positional arguments "provider", "registry" in call to "load_adapter_stack"  [call-arg]
scripts/validate_oracles.py:170: error: Missing positional arguments "provider", "registry" in call to "load_adapter_stack"  [call-arg]
scripts/validate_oracles.py:172: error: "object" not callable  [operator]
scripts/validate_oracles.py:178: error: "object" not callable  [operator]
```

Working directory: `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune`.

## Context

`evaluation.benchmarks.adapter_stack.load_adapter_stack` requires 4 args: `base_model`, `adapter_ids`, `provider`, `registry`. The script currently calls it with only the first two — a pre-existing bug from gap 4 (commit `98b8713`), undetected because the test suite monkeypatches `_evaluate_one` away.

The `run_benchmark` "object not callable" errors at lines 172/178 are a known INFRA-05 limitation (mypy can't resolve deferred imports inside function bodies).

## Reference — existing pattern

`scripts/evaluate_round2.py` (commit `eb4b38c`, fixed with the same constraint earlier) does it correctly:

```python
def _open_registry(url: str) -> Any:
    from sqlmodel import create_engine  # noqa: PLC0415
    from adapter_registry.registry import AdapterRegistry  # noqa: PLC0415
    engine = create_engine(url)
    return AdapterRegistry(engine=engine)

# Inside the main worker:
from inference.factory import get_provider  # noqa: PLC0415
provider = get_provider()
stack = load_adapter_stack(
    base_model=base_model,
    adapter_ids=[adapter_id],
    provider=provider,
    registry=registry,
)
```

## Fix

Read `scripts/validate_oracles.py` first. Apply these changes:

### 1. Add CLI arg `--oracle-registry-url`

In `_build_parser()`, add a new argument (place it right after `--base-model`):

```python
    parser.add_argument(
        "--oracle-registry-url",
        default="",
        help=(
            "SQLAlchemy URL for the AdapterRegistry SQLite DB holding the "
            "oracles. Required unless --dry-run. Example: "
            "sqlite:///~/.rune/adapters.db"
        ),
    )
```

### 2. Thread `oracle_registry_url` through `validate_oracles` and `_evaluate_one`

Add `oracle_registry_url: str = ""` kwarg to `validate_oracles` (keyword-only, before `dry_run`). Thread it into the `_evaluate_one(...)` call. Add a guard: when not `dry_run` and `oracle_registry_url` is empty, raise `ValueError("oracle_registry_url is required when not dry-run")`.

Update `main()` to pass `oracle_registry_url=args.oracle_registry_url` into `validate_oracles`.

### 3. Rewrite `_evaluate_one` to use the real API

Add `oracle_registry_url: str` parameter (keyword-only after existing ones). Rewrite body to open registry, get provider, and pass them plus `# type: ignore[operator]` on `run_benchmark`:

```python
def _evaluate_one(
    base_model: str,
    bin_key: str,
    benchmark: str,
    adapter_id: str,
    threshold: float,
    max_samples: int | None,
    timeout_s: int,
    workers: int,
    oracle_registry_url: str,
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Evaluate a single oracle against the base. Heavy imports deferred."""
    from adapter_registry.registry import AdapterRegistry  # noqa: PLC0415
    from evaluation.benchmarks import BenchmarkConfig, run_benchmark  # noqa: PLC0415
    from evaluation.benchmarks.adapter_stack import load_adapter_stack  # noqa: PLC0415
    from inference.factory import get_provider  # noqa: PLC0415
    from sqlmodel import create_engine  # noqa: PLC0415

    cfg = BenchmarkConfig(
        timeout_s=timeout_s,
        max_workers=workers,
        max_samples=max_samples,
    )

    engine = create_engine(oracle_registry_url)
    registry = AdapterRegistry(engine=engine)
    provider = get_provider()

    base_stack = load_adapter_stack(
        base_model=base_model,
        adapter_ids=[],
        provider=provider,
        registry=registry,
    )
    stack = load_adapter_stack(
        base_model=base_model,
        adapter_ids=[adapter_id],
        provider=provider,
        registry=registry,
    )

    base_result = run_benchmark(  # type: ignore[operator]
        adapter_stack=base_stack,
        benchmark_id=benchmark,
        max_samples=max_samples,
        config=cfg,
    )
    stack_result = run_benchmark(  # type: ignore[operator]
        adapter_stack=stack,
        benchmark_id=benchmark,
        max_samples=max_samples,
        config=cfg,
    )
    delta = stack_result.pass_at_1 - base_result.pass_at_1
    return {
        "bin_key": bin_key,
        "benchmark": benchmark,
        "adapter_id": adapter_id,
        "base_pass_at_1": base_result.pass_at_1,
        "stack_pass_at_1": stack_result.pass_at_1,
        "delta": delta,
        "threshold": threshold,
        "passed": delta >= threshold,
        "dry_run": False,
    }
```

## Verification

- `uv run mypy scripts/validate_oracles.py` → `Success: no issues found in 1 source file`.
- `uv run mypy libs/ services/` → clean (no new errors introduced).
- `uv run ruff check scripts/validate_oracles.py` → clean.
- `uv run pytest tests/test_validate_oracles.py -v` → existing tests pass (they monkeypatch `_evaluate_one` so the signature change is invisible to them, EXCEPT the `oracle_registry_url` validation path. If `test_dry_run_produces_stub_results` passes without setting `oracle_registry_url`, the dry-run path must continue to work with the default empty string. The validation guard only fires when NOT dry_run, so this should still pass).

## Commit

```bash
git add scripts/validate_oracles.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(scripts): use real load_adapter_stack API + add registry CLI arg (PR #28)

validate_oracles.py called load_adapter_stack with only base_model and
adapter_ids, missing the required provider and registry args — a
pre-existing bug from gap 4 (commit 98b8713) undetected because the
test suite monkeypatches _evaluate_one. Tripped mypy in the PR #28 CI.

Mirrors the pattern landed in scripts/evaluate_round2.py (commit
eb4b38c): adds --oracle-registry-url CLI arg, opens registry +
InferenceProvider inside _evaluate_one, passes both to
load_adapter_stack. Suppresses two deferred-import "object not callable"
mypy false positives on run_benchmark calls (INFRA-05 limitation).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

Don't touch `docs/superpowers/` deletions.

[project]
name = "rune"
version = "0.1.0"
description = "Local-first coding agent with LoRA weight-space episodic memory"
readme = "README.md"
requires-python = ">=3.12,<3.13"
dependencies = [
    "aiohttp>=3.10.0",
    "httpx>=0.28.1",
    "markdown>=3.4.0",
    "playwright>=1.40.0",
    "mkdocs>=1.6.1",
    "mkdocs-monorepo-plugin>=1.0.0",
    "mkdocstrings[python]>=0.24.0",
    "requests>=2.32.5",
    "tenacity>=8.2.0",
    "pytest-asyncio>=1.3.0",
    "aioresponses>=0.7.8",
    "fastapi>=0.128.0",
    "sqlalchemy>=2.0.45",
    "sqlmodel>=0.0.31",
    "python-dotenv>=1.2.1",
    "diskcache>=5.6.3",
    "platformdirs>=4.5.1",
    "langchain>=1.2.6",
    "langgraph>=1.0.6",
    "python-multipart>=0.0.21",
    "alembic>=1.13.0",
    "pymdown-extensions>=10.21",
    "openai>=1.0.0",
    "psutil>=5.9.0",
    "llama-cpp-python>=0.3.0",
    "safetensors>=0.7.0",
    "peft>=0.15",
    "transformers>=4.57,<4.58",
    "huggingface-hub>=0.20.0",
    "ctx-to-lora>=0.0.1",
    "einops>=0.8.2",
    "jaxtyping>=0.3.9",
    "datasets>=4.6.1",
    "torch>=2.11.0,<2.12",
]

[project.optional-dependencies]
gpu = [
    "bitsandbytes>=0.45.0",
    "trl>=0.16.0",
    "flash-attn>=2.8.0",
    "flash-attn-3>=3.0.0",
]
eval = [
    "evalplus>=0.3.0",
]

[dependency-groups]
dev = [
    "mypy>=1.19.0",
    "pytest>=9.0.2",
    "pytest-asyncio>=1.2.0",
    "pytest-cov>=6.0.0",
    "ruff>=0.14.8",
    "aioresponses>=0.7.8",
    "mlflow>=3.8.1",
    "pytest-xdist>=3.8.0",
    "requests-mock>=1.12.1",
    "pandas-stubs>=3.0.0.260204",
]

[tool.uv.sources]
ctx-to-lora = { git = "https://github.com/SakanaAI/doc-to-lora.git" }
flash-attn = { url = "https://github.com/mjun0812/flash-attention-prebuild-wheels/releases/download/v0.9.4/flash_attn-2.8.3+cu130torch2.11-cp312-cp312-linux_x86_64.whl" }
flash-attn-3 = { url = "https://github.com/mjun0812/flash-attention-prebuild-wheels/releases/download/v0.9.4/flash_attn_3-3.0.0+cu130torch2.11gite2743ab-cp39-abi3-linux_x86_64.whl" }

[[tool.uv.dependency-metadata]]
name = "ctx-to-lora"
version = "0.0.1"
requires-dist = []  # ignore Sakana's pinned deps (vllm, torch==2.6, transformers==4.51)

[tool.uv.workspace]
members = [
    "services/api-service",
    "services/evolution-svc",
    "services/rune-agent",
    "services/training-svc",
    "libs/evaluation",
    "libs/events-py",
    "libs/inference",
    "libs/model-training",
    "libs/shared",
    "libs/adapter-registry",
    "libs/corpus-producer",
]

[tool.uv]
no-build-isolation-package = ["setuptools"]

[tool.ruff]
target-version = "py312"
line-length = 88
exclude = ["venv", ".venv", "docs", "site", ".planning"]

[tool.ruff.lint]
select = ["E", "F", "W", "C", "N", "I", "D"]
ignore = ["E203", "D203", "D213", "D100", "D413", "D104"]
pydocstyle.convention = "google"

[tool.ruff.lint.per-file-ignores]
"tests/**" = ["D"]
"**/tests/**" = ["D"]
"conftest.py" = ["D"]
"**/conftest.py" = ["D"]
"scripts/**" = ["D", "C901", "E501", "E402"]
"**/scripts/**" = ["D", "C901", "E501", "E402"]

[tool.mypy]
exclude = "^(site|services/.*/tests|libs/.*/tests|instructions|notebooks|scripts/eval|services/lora-server)/"

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "fastapi"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "pydantic"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "yaml"
ignore_missing_imports = true

module = "sqlalchemy"
ignore_missing_imports = true

ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]

ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = "httpx"
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [
    "boto3",
    "boto3.*",
    "botocore",
    "botocore.*",
]
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [
    "fitz",
    "fitz.*",
]
ignore_missing_imports = true

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [

]

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [

[[tool.mypy.overrides]]
module = [
    "bitsandbytes",

    "run_training_hpo",
    "pandas",
    "pandas.*",
]
ignore_missing_imports = true

[tool.pytest.ini_options]
testpaths = ["services", "libs", "tests"]
norecursedirs = ["site", "docs", ".venv", "venv", ".uv_cache", "node_modules", ".git"]
markers = [
    "e2e: end-to-end API workflow tests",
    "integration: marks tests as integration tests",
    "requires_gpu: test requires GPU libraries (torch, peft, trl)",
]
filterwarnings = [
    "ignore:builtin type swigvarlink has no __module__ attribute:DeprecationWarning",
    "ignore:builtin type SwigPyPacked has no __module__ attribute:DeprecationWarning",
    "ignore:builtin type SwigPyObject has no __module__ attribute:DeprecationWarning",
    "ignore:unclosed database:ResourceWarning",
    "ignore::pytest.PytestUnraisableExceptionWarning",
    "ignore:No data was collected:coverage.exceptions.CoverageWarning",
    "ignore:Use 'content=<...>' to upload raw bytes:DeprecationWarning:httpx",
    "ignore:`torch.jit.script_method` is deprecated:DeprecationWarning",
]
pythonpath = [
    "services/api-service/src",
    "services/evolution-svc/src",
    "services/rune-agent/src",
    "services/training-svc/src",
    "libs/adapter-registry/src",
    "libs/corpus-producer/src",
    "libs/evaluation/src",
    "libs/events-py/src",
    "libs/inference/src",
    "libs/model-training/src",
    "libs/shared/src",
]
asyncio_mode = "auto"
# importlib mode allows multiple test files with the same basename across
# different directories without __init__.py (required for pytest-xdist)
addopts = "-n auto --dist loadgroup --import-mode=importlib"

[tool.coverage.run]
source = [
    "services/api-service/src",

]
omit = ["*/tests/*", "*/.venv/*", "*/venv/*"]

[tool.coverage.report]
exclude_lines = [
    "pragma: no cover",
    "def __repr__",
    "raise AssertionError",
    "raise NotImplementedError",
    "if __name__ == .__main__.:",
    "if TYPE_CHECKING:",
]

file.content:
"""Tests for corpus_producer.models.PhaseArtifact."""

from corpus_producer.models import PhaseArtifact


def _make(phase: str = "decompose", benchmark: str = "humaneval") -> PhaseArtifact:
    return PhaseArtifact(
        phase=phase,
        benchmark=benchmark,
        problem_id="HumanEval/0",
        pipeline_run_id="run-abc",
        input_text="## Task\nWrite a function.",
        output_text="1. subtask_a — parse input",
        pass_at_1=True,
    )


def test_bin_key_non_diagnose():
    art = _make(phase="decompose", benchmark="humaneval")
    assert art.bin_key() == "decompose_humaneval"


def test_bin_key_diagnose_always_pooled():
    art = _make(phase="diagnose", benchmark="mbpp")
    assert art.bin_key() == "diagnose_pooled"


def test_to_manifest_record_keys():
    art = _make()
    rec = art.to_manifest_record()
    assert "task_id" in rec
    assert "activation_text" in rec
    assert "teacher_text" in rec
    assert "metadata" in rec


def test_to_manifest_record_task_id_format():
    art = _make(phase="plan", benchmark="mbpp")
    art.problem_id = "MBPP/1"
    rec = art.to_manifest_record()
    assert rec["task_id"] == "mbpp/MBPP/1/plan"


def test_to_manifest_record_teacher_contains_output():
    art = _make()
    rec = art.to_manifest_record()
    assert art.output_text in rec["teacher_text"]


def test_to_manifest_record_metadata_provenance():
    art = _make(phase="code", benchmark="apps")
    art.rationalized = True
    rec = art.to_manifest_record()
    assert rec["metadata"]["rationalized"] is True
    assert rec["metadata"]["phase"] == "code"


def test_rationalized_defaults_false():
    art = _make()
    assert art.rationalized is False


def test_metadata_extra_fields_forwarded():
    art = _make()
    art.metadata["subtask"] = "parse_input"
    rec = art.to_manifest_record()
    assert rec["metadata"]["subtask"] == "parse_input"

file.content:
changed_weight: Weight for changed / inserted tokens.
        unchanged_weight: Weight for unchanged (context) tokens.
        tokenizer: Optional tokenizer passed to :class:`DiffWeightedDataCollator`
            to enable the hunk path.  Falls back to ``processing_class`` if
            ``tokenizer`` is ``None`` and ``processing_class`` is provided.

    Returns:
        A configured ``trl.SFTTrainer`` instance.
    """
    from trl import DataCollatorForCompletionOnlyLM, SFTTrainer

    resolved_tokenizer = tokenizer if tokenizer is not None else processing_class

    inner_collator = DataCollatorForCompletionOnlyLM(
        response_template="<|im_start|>assistant",
        tokenizer=resolved_tokenizer,
    )
    collator = DiffWeightedDataCollator(
        inner_collator,
        changed_weight=changed_weight,
        unchanged_weight=unchanged_weight,
        tokenizer=resolved_tokenizer,
    )

    return SFTTrainer(

content.text: These subprocess tests were in the `skipped` tier on prior CI runs (paths-filter) and are now triggered. Root cause: subprocess doesn't inherit pytest's pythonpath so `python -m model_training.*` fails. Fixing:

input.description: Fix subprocess PYTHONPATH in CLI tests
input.prompt:
Fix 5 CI test failures caused by subprocess tests not inheriting pytest's pythonpath.

Working directory: `/Users/noahdolevelixir/Code/rune`.

## Failing tests

All in:
- `libs/model-training/tests/test_encoder_cli.py` (4 tests)
- `libs/model-training/tests/test_reconstruction_cli.py` (1 test)

Each calls `subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "model_training.<submodule>.cli", ...])` which fails in CI with `ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'model_training'` because the subprocess inherits `.venv/bin/python` but not the pytest pythonpath that makes `libs/model-training/src` + `libs/shared/src` importable.

Tests pass locally via `uv run pytest` because the uv-managed env has the workspace installed; CI's `.venv` doesn't.

## Fix

Read both files first. Then for every `subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "model_training.*", ...])` call, add an `env=` kwarg that extends `PYTHONPATH` with the workspace source paths.

Recommended approach: add a small helper at the top of each test file that constructs the env:

```python
import os
import subprocess
import sys
from pathlib import Path

_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3]
_SRC_PATHS = [
    str(_REPO_ROOT / "libs" / "model-training" / "src"),
    str(_REPO_ROOT / "libs" / "shared" / "src"),
    str(_REPO_ROOT / "libs" / "adapter-registry" / "src"),
    str(_REPO_ROOT / "libs" / "inference" / "src"),
]


def _subprocess_env() -> dict[str, str]:
    """Return env dict with PYTHONPATH covering the workspace source tree.

    Subprocess `python -m model_training.*` calls rely on this because
    the runner's .venv may not have the workspace installed in
    site-packages (only via pytest's pythonpath config).
    """
    env = os.environ.copy()
    existing = env.get("PYTHONPATH", "")
    extra = os.pathsep.join(_SRC_PATHS)
    env["PYTHONPATH"] = f"{extra}{os.pathsep}{existing}" if existing else extra
    return env
```

Then add `env=_subprocess_env()` to every `subprocess.run(...)` call in the file.

**Important:** `parents[3]` assumes the test file is at `libs/model-training/tests/test_*.py` (4 levels up from `test_*.py` → `tests/` → `model-training/` → `libs/` → repo root). Verify with a `print(_REPO_ROOT)` mentally or one-off run.

For `test_reconstruction_cli.py`, only 1 subprocess call is failing (the dry-run subprocess one). Use the same approach.

If a test file has multiple subprocess calls, add `env=_subprocess_env()` to all of them for consistency.

## Verification

- `uv run pytest libs/model-training/tests/test_encoder_cli.py libs/model-training/tests/test_reconstruction_cli.py -v` → all pass locally (they already did — make sure no regression).
- `uv run ruff check libs/model-training/tests/test_encoder_cli.py libs/model-training/tests/test_reconstruction_cli.py` → clean.
- `uv run ruff format --check libs/model-training/tests/test_encoder_cli.py libs/model-training/tests/test_reconstruction_cli.py` → clean.

## Commit

Stage only the 2 test files:

```bash
git add libs/model-training/tests/test_encoder_cli.py libs/model-training/tests/test_reconstruction_cli.py
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
test(training): thread PYTHONPATH into CLI subprocess calls for CI

CI `test` job was failing on 5 tests (4 in test_encoder_cli.py, 1 in
test_reconstruction_cli.py) because their subprocess.run([sys.executable,
"-m", "model_training.*", ...]) calls inherit .venv/bin/python but not
pytest's pythonpath configuration, producing
"ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'model_training'".

Added a _subprocess_env() helper to each file that extends PYTHONPATH
with libs/model-training/src, libs/shared/src, libs/adapter-registry/src,
and libs/inference/src. Tests pass locally (uv-managed env already has
the workspace on path) AND in CI (explicit PYTHONPATH override).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
```

Don't touch anything else. Report: status, commit SHA, test count, any concerns.

content.text:
Done.

**Status:** committed as `6c475b8`

**What changed:**
- Both test files got a module-level `_REPO_ROOT` / `_SRC_PATHS` block and a `_subprocess_env()` helper (identical in both).
- `test_encoder_cli.py`: all 4 `subprocess.run` calls now pass `env=_subprocess_env()`.
- `test_reconstruction_cli.py`: the 1 `subprocess.run` call (`test_dry_run_subprocess_does_not_import_torch`) now passes `env=_subprocess_env()`.
- 9/9 tests pass, ruff clean, format clean.

**No concerns.** The `parents[3]` depth is correct: `test_*.py` → `tests/` → `model-training/` → `libs/` → repo root.
